name: prisma
context: Prisma ORM for Node.js and TypeScript — schema-first database access, migrations, and type-safe queries
info: Prisma is the most popular Node.js/TypeScript ORM. It provides a declarative schema language for defining data models, an automated migration system for evolving database structure, and a fully type-safe query client generated from your schema. Prisma supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, SQL Server, MongoDB, and CockroachDB. Agents use Prisma skills to define schemas, run migrations, generate the client, write type-safe queries with relations, seed databases, and debug common errors. The ecosystem is organized around the core workflow: schema → migrate → generate → query.
body:
# Prisma ORM

Prisma is a next-generation ORM for Node.js and TypeScript that replaces traditional ORMs with a schema-first, type-safe approach to database access.

## Core Workflow

```
schema.prisma → prisma migrate dev → prisma generate → PrismaClient queries
```

1. **[[prisma/schema]]** — Define your data model in `prisma/schema.prisma`
2. **[[prisma/migrate]]** — Generate and apply SQL migrations from schema changes
3. **[[prisma/client]]** — Generate a type-safe client, then query your database
4. **[[prisma/seed]]** — Populate your database with initial or test data

## Quick Start

```bash
# Initialize Prisma in an existing project
npm install prisma --save-dev
npm install @prisma/client
npx prisma init --datasource-provider postgresql

# After editing schema.prisma:
npx prisma migrate dev --name init
npx prisma generate
```

## Architecture

```
prisma/
  schema.prisma          # Data model, datasource, generators
  migrations/            # SQL migration files (managed by prisma migrate)
    migration_lock.toml  # Provider lock
    20240101_init/
      migration.sql
  seed.ts                # Database seeding script
```

## Supported Databases

| Provider | Value | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|
| PostgreSQL | `postgresql` | Full feature support |
| MySQL | `mysql` | Full feature support |
| SQLite | `sqlite` | File-based, great for dev |
| SQL Server | `sqlserver` | Microsoft SQL Server |
| MongoDB | `mongodb` | Document model, uses `db push` instead of migrate |
| CockroachDB | `cockroachdb` | PostgreSQL-compatible |

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose | Example |
|----------|---------|--------|
| `DATABASE_URL` | Connection string | `postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/mydb?schema=public` |

Connection string format: `provider://user:password@host:port/database?schema=public`

## Key Packages

| Package | Purpose | Install |
|---------|---------|--------|
| `prisma` | CLI and migration engine | `npm i -D prisma` |
| `@prisma/client` | Runtime query client | `npm i @prisma/client` |

## Tools

- **[[prisma/studio]]** — Browser-based GUI for viewing and editing data
- **[[prisma/troubleshooting]]** — Error codes, common issues, and fixes

sections.title: Quick start
sections.body: Install `prisma` (dev) and `@prisma/client` (runtime). Run `npx prisma init --datasource-provider postgresql` to scaffold. Edit `prisma/schema.prisma`, then `npx prisma migrate dev --name init` to create your first migration. Use `npx prisma generate` to regenerate the client after schema changes.

sections.title: Database providers
sections.body: Set the `provider` field in the `datasource` block: `postgresql`, `mysql`, `sqlite`, `sqlserver`, `mongodb`, `cockroachdb`. Each provider has its own native type annotations (e.g., `@db.VarChar(255)` for PostgreSQL). MongoDB uses `db push` instead of `migrate dev` for schema management.

sections.title: CLI commands overview
sections.body: Key commands: `npx prisma init` (scaffold), `npx prisma generate` (build client), `npx prisma migrate dev` (create+apply migration), `npx prisma migrate deploy` (apply in production), `npx prisma db push` (prototype without migration files), `npx prisma db pull` (introspect existing DB), `npx prisma db seed` (run seed script), `npx prisma studio` (GUI), `npx prisma validate` (check schema), `npx prisma format` (format schema).

links.context: Define data models, datasources, and generators

Browser GUI for viewing and editing data

related.note: Prisma's type-safe client requires TypeScript for full benefit
Prisma is the most common ORM used with Next.js projects
